content.title: The First Pledge of Aqabah
content.subtitle: A secret midnight meeting in the valley of Aqabah became the first spark that illuminated
content.body:
Through the suffocating darkness of Makkah, a miraculous light quietly emerged within the narrow valley of Aqabah. During the crowded season of Hajj, twelve young men from Yathrib (Madinah) secretly crossed the mountain paths under the cover of night, escaping the watchful eyes of Quraysh. Exhausted by tribal wars, bloodshed, and the emptiness of Jahiliyyah, their hearts longed for truth.

There, in the silence of the night, they met Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. As he recited the powerful verses of the Qur’an, the message of Tawhid shook their souls and shattered the darkness of idol worship that had ruled Arabia for centuries.

That night, they pledged their hands to the Prophet ﷺ in what history remembers as The First Pledge of Aqabah — not a political alliance, but a covenant of faith, morality, and spiritual rebirth.

They vowed:

  • To worship Allah alone,

  • To abandon theft and immorality,

  • To never kill their children out of poverty,

  • To avoid false accusations,

  • And to obey the Prophet ﷺ in every righteous matter.

While Makkah slept in silence, a new chapter of history was being written in Aqabah — a moment that would soon bring the first dawn of iman to Madinah and transform the course of the world forever.

References

  • Sahih al-Bukhari — Hadith 3999

  • Sahih Muslim — Hadith 1709

  • Sirat Ibn Hisham

  • Ar-Raheeq Al-Makhtum
